semaglutide.titration: Increase every 4 weeks as tolerated: 0.25 → 0.5 → 1.0 → 1.7 → 2.4 mg
semaglutide.maintenance: 1.7–2.4 mg weekly (titrate to tolerability and response)
semaglutide.notes: Slow titration limits GI side effects. Hold escalation if significant nausea. Reassess after 12 weeks at target dose; consider continuation if ≥5% weight loss.

semaglutide.starting: 0.25 mg weekly x4 weeks (non-therapeutic, for tolerability)
semaglutide.titration: 0.5 mg weekly; may increase to 1.0 then 2.0 mg
semaglutide.notes: Oral semaglutide (Rybelsus) alternative: 3 mg x30d → 7 mg → 14 mg daily, taken fasting with ≤4 oz water, 30 min before food/other meds.

tirzepatide.titration: Increase by 2.5 mg every 4 weeks as tolerated: 2.5 → 5 → 7.5 → 10 → 12.5 → 15 mg
tirzepatide.maintenance: 5–15 mg weekly (lowest effective dose maintaining response)
tirzepatide.notes: Mean weight loss ~15–22% at higher doses. Titrate to tolerability; many patients maintain at 5–10 mg.

tirzepatide.titration: 5 mg weekly; increase by 2.5 mg q4wk as needed
tirzepatide.notes: Superior HbA1c reduction vs semaglutide in head-to-head data.

liraglutide.notes: Daily dosing; discontinue if <4% weight loss by 16 weeks.

liraglutide.titration: 1.2 mg daily; may increase to 1.8 mg

naltrexone-bupropion.titration: Week 1: 1 tab AM. Week 2: 1 tab BID. Week 3: 2 tabs AM, 1 tab PM. Week 4+: 2 tabs BID
naltrexone-bupropion.maintenance: 2 tablets twice daily (32 mg naltrexone / 360 mg bupropion total/day)
naltrexone-bupropion.notes: Avoid high-fat meals (raises bupropion absorption/seizure risk). Discontinue if <5% weight loss by week 12 at maintenance.

bpc-157.evidenceGrade: D (preclinical / anecdotal; no large human RCTs)
bpc-157.notes: Doses are empiric/protocol-based — not FDA-established. Typical cycles 4–8 weeks. Inform patient of investigational status.
Oral route favored for GI-localized indications.

sermorelin.titration: Up to 300–500 mcg nightly as tolerated
sermorelin.notes: Dose on empty stomach at night to align with natural GH pulse. Cycle and reassess IGF-1. Avoid carbohydrate near dosing (blunts GH release).

cjc-1295-ipamorelin.starting: CJC-1295 (no DAC) 100 mcg + ipamorelin 100–200 mcg nightly
cjc-1295-ipamorelin.titration: Up to 200/200–300 mcg; some protocols 5 days on / 2 off
cjc-1295-ipamorelin.notes: Dose fasting at night. Monitor IGF-1 to keep in age-appropriate upper range, not supraphysiologic. Cycle 3–6 months with reassessment.

ipamorelin.titration: 100–300 mcg per dose, 1–3x/day in some protocols
ipamorelin.notes: Selective GHRP; commonly paired with a GHRH analog for synergy.

tesamorelin.indication: Visceral adipose tissue reduction / HIV lipodystrophy
tesamorelin.evidenceGrade: A for HIV lipodystrophy (FDA-approved); off-label otherwise
tesamorelin.notes: Reassess IGF-1; effect on VAT typically evident by 3–6 months. Discontinue if no response.

thymosin-beta-4.titration: Loading ~2–2.5 mg 2x/week x4–6 weeks, then maintenance
thymosin-beta-4.notes: Investigational. Often combined with BPC-157. Inform patient of non-FDA-approved status.

testosterone-cypionate.starting: 100 mg per week (often split, e.g. 50 mg twice weekly SC)
testosterone-cypionate.titration: Adjust to mid-normal trough total testosterone (~500–700 ng/dL) and symptoms
testosterone-cypionate.notes: Twice-weekly or every-other-day SC dosing smooths levels and limits erythrocytosis/estradiol spikes vs large q2-week IM boluses. Target trough mid-normal range; check level before next dose.

testosterone-cream.titration: Adjust to mid-normal total testosterone and symptoms
testosterone-cream.notes: Apply to clean dry skin (shoulders/upper arms or scrotal — scrotal needs lower dose). Avoid skin-to-skin transfer to women/children. Check level 2–4 weeks after start.

testosterone-cream.titration: Adjust to keep total testosterone in upper-normal female range
testosterone-cream.evidenceGrade: B (off-label; ISSWSH guidance supports low-dose for HSDD)
testosterone-cream.notes: Goal is physiologic female range — not male levels. Monitor for androgenic effects (acne, hirsutism, voice). Recheck level at 3–6 weeks.

estradiol.titration: Adjust to symptom relief at lowest effective dose
estradiol.notes: Transdermal preferred — lower VTE/stroke risk than oral. ALWAYS pair with progesterone if uterus is intact. Initiate within 10 years of menopause / before age 60 for best benefit-risk.

progesterone.indication: Endometrial protection with estrogen / perimenopausal symptoms
progesterone.starting: 100 mg nightly (continuous) or 200 mg nightly x12 days/month (cyclic)
progesterone.titration: 200 mg nightly for sleep/symptom benefit if tolerated
progesterone.notes: Bedtime dosing leverages sedative allopregnanolone effect. Continuous regimen avoids monthly bleeding. Transdermal cream is insufficient for endometrial protection — use oral when opposing estrogen.

dhea.titration: Adjust to DHEA-S in mid-normal age range
dhea.notes: Recheck DHEA-S (and downstream estradiol/testosterone) at 6–8 weeks. Lower doses in women to limit acne/hirsutism. Vaginal prasterone 6.5 mg nightly for GSM.

anastrozole.starting: 0.25 mg once or twice weekly (only if estradiol confirmed elevated and symptomatic)
anastrozole.titration: Adjust to keep estradiol mid-normal — do NOT crash it
anastrozole.notes: Avoid over-suppression — low estradiol harms libido, mood, lipids, and bone in men. First optimize T dose/frequency before adding an AI. Recheck estradiol in 2–4 weeks.

pregnenolone.notes: Morning dosing (can be activating). Monitor downstream hormones in comprehensive regimens.

hcg.titration: Adjust to maintain testicular volume / intratesticular T; up to 500 IU 2–3x/week
hcg.notes: Preserves spermatogenesis and testicular size during exogenous androgen therapy. Monitor estradiol (hCG can raise it).
Stimulates endogenous testosterone; option for men prioritizing fertility over exogenous T.

nad-plus.starting: IV 250 mg over 2–4 hours, or SC 50–100 mg
nad-plus.titration: IV up to 500–750 mg per session as tolerated; SC daily/several times weekly
nad-plus.maintenance: IV 250–500 mg every 1–4 weeks, or SC 50–100 mg several times weekly
nad-plus.max: ~1000 mg IV per session (rate-limited by tolerance)
nad-plus.notes: Infuse SLOWLY — rapid IV causes flushing, nausea, chest/abdominal tightness; slow the rate to abort. Loading series (e.g. several sessions over 1–2 weeks) then maintenance is common.

metformin.starting: 500 mg once daily with the evening meal
metformin.titration: Increase by 500 mg weekly as tolerated
metformin.notes: Extended-release improves GI tolerance. Take with food. Hold for iodinated contrast and major surgery; verify eGFR ≥30.
May blunt exercise-induced mitochondrial adaptations at higher doses — consider timing away from training. Monitor B12 on long-term use.

rapamycin.titration: Some protocols 3–6 mg weekly, occasionally up to 8 mg weekly, guided by tolerability/biomarkers
rapamycin.max: ~8 mg once weekly (off-label longevity ceiling varies by clinician)
rapamycin.evidenceGrade: C (robust animal data; human longevity RCTs ongoing/limited)
rapamycin.notes: Intermittent weekly dosing biases toward mTORC1 inhibition and limits immunosuppression vs daily transplant dosing. Hold around infections, surgery, and live vaccines. Monitor lipids, glucose, CBC.

berberine.starting: 500 mg once daily with a meal
berberine.notes: Divide doses with meals to limit GI upset. Strong CYP3A4/P-gp inhibitor — screen interactions. Dihydroberberine allows lower dosing.

resveratrol.notes: Take with dietary fat for absorption. Often paired with NMN/NAD+. Mild antiplatelet effect — caution with anticoagulants.

nmn.evidenceGrade: B (raises NAD+ in human studies; outcomes emerging)
nmn.notes: Morning dosing aligns with circadian NAD+ rhythm. Often combined with TMG (methyl donor) and resveratrol.

pt-141.starting: 0.5–1 mg SC ~45 min to a few hours before activity
pt-141.titration: Up to 1.75 mg (Vyleesi dose) based on response/tolerability
pt-141.maintenance: On-demand; no more than one dose per 24h and ≤8 doses/month
pt-141.evidenceGrade: A for premenopausal HSDD (FDA-approved Vyleesi); off-label in men
pt-141.notes: Start low to assess nausea (common) and transient BP rise. Avoid in uncontrolled hypertension/CVD. Do not exceed one dose/24h.

oxytocin.starting: Sublingual 10–20 IU (or ~20–40 IU) ~30–60 min before activity
oxytocin.titration: Adjust within ~10–80 IU range by response
oxytocin.maintenance: On-demand or regular low-dose per protocol
oxytocin.notes: Compounded sublingual/nasal only — not IV. Avoid in pregnancy (uterotonic).

tadalafil.titration: Increase to 5 mg daily based on response
tadalafil.notes: Daily dosing allows spontaneity and treats LUTS/BPH. Absolutely avoid with nitrates.
Onset ~30–60 min, effect up to 36h. Reduce dose with potent CYP3A4 inhibitors.

sildenafil.notes: High-fat meals delay onset. Start 25 mg with CYP3A4 inhibitors, age >65, or renal/hepatic impairment. Absolutely avoid with nitrates.

thymosin-alpha-1.titration: 1.6 mg 2–3x/week; intensive protocols use daily short courses
thymosin-alpha-1.notes: Dosing mirrors approved Zadaxin regimens. Avoid in transplant immunosuppression and active autoimmune flares.

glutathione.route: IV push, IM, or oral liposomal
glutathione.starting: IV 600–1200 mg per session; oral liposomal 250–500 mg/day
glutathione.titration: IV up to 2000 mg per session; 1–2 sessions/week
glutathione.notes: IV push given slowly. Avoid nebulized form in asthmatics (bronchospasm). Pair with vitamin C in many protocols.

low-dose-naltrexone.titration: Increase by 1.5 mg every 1–2 weeks to target
low-dose-naltrexone.notes: Must be opioid-free (precipitates withdrawal). Vivid dreams/sleep disturbance early — try AM dosing if so. Allow 6–8 weeks for effect.

finasteride.titration: None typically (1 mg is standard); some use 0.5 mg or topical
finasteride.max: 1 mg/day for alopecia (5 mg is BPH dose)
finasteride.notes: Effect takes 3–6 months; benefit lost ~6–12 months after stopping. Topical compounding reduces systemic DHT suppression. Teratogenic — women of childbearing potential must not handle.
Reduces PSA ~50% — double measured PSA when screening. Effect on symptoms over 3–6 months.

oral-minoxidil.starting: Women 0.625–1.25 mg daily; Men 1.25–2.5 mg daily
oral-minoxidil.titration: Increase gradually to 2.5–5 mg as tolerated
oral-minoxidil.notes: Monitor for hypertrichosis, ankle edema, lightheadedness, tachycardia. Start low; effect over 3–6 months. Consider baseline BP/HR.

ghk-cu.starting: Apply 1–2% serum/cream once or twice daily
ghk-cu.notes: Avoid layering directly with high-dose vitamin C (can interact). Most robust evidence is topical.
Investigational injectable use. Avoid in Wilson's disease / copper disorders.

tretinoin.titration: Increase frequency to nightly and strength (0.05 → 0.1%) as tolerated over weeks
tretinoin.notes: Apply to dry skin; 'retinization' irritation expected early — buffer with moisturizer and reduce frequency if needed. Daily SPF mandatory. Avoid in pregnancy.
